channel.title: Pike Place Market Events
channel.description: Upcoming events for Pike Place Market Events

item.title: The Original Food and Culture Tour of Pike Place Market
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Pike Place Market

VOTED 10 BEST FOOD TOURS IN THE COUNTRY BY USA TODAY! Join Savor Seattle for Seattle’s original food and cultural tour of Pike Place Market! On their signature tour, they will take you on a journey through the lively and bustling days in the heart of Seattle: Pike Place Market. While their guides give you the most delicious tastings at eight different partners. You’ll also learn the history of the Market and hear the stories of the small-batch food and craft producers that make this place one of Seattle’s most beloved landmarks. Find out why fish fly, why a pig is our mascot, how the “Original” Starbucks was founded, how an alleyway became covered in gum, what made Pike Place Market one of Anthony Bourdain’s favorite places, and many more pieces of info Seattle Savor guides we’ll be thrilled to share with you while you savor culinary offerings. Once you’re done, you’ll be equipped and ready to dive deeper into the historic neighborhood. Tours are available daily, starting at 10am and 2

Event details

item.title: Market to Table Tour & Cooking Class
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Atrium Kitchen, 93 Pike St, Seattle, WA 98101

“We wish we did this at the beginning of our trip!” “You’ve exceeded all of our expectations! And then some!!!” … These are some of the frequent comments we receive during our Market to Table experience!   Reserve Your Spot! Each experience INCLUDES: • 90-120 minutes VIP TOUR of Pike Place Market, visiting Chef Traci’s favorite spots, and includes some delightful sips and nibbles • 60-90 minute HANDS-ON cooking class (but if you want to sit and watch, you can do that too!) • 30-60 minute LUNCH or DINNER • All welcome nibbles, appetizers, non alcoholic beverages, tastes & sips along the tour, all ingredients for a customized cooking class • Estimate 3-4 hours for the complete experience • All class menus are created at the start of class and take into consideration individual dietary preferences and allergies (please note: the kitchen is not a gluten-free, nut-free facility, however menus may be customized to omit nuts, gluten, and/or dairy) The Market to Table tour and cooking cla

Event details

item.title: Duo Comedy Improv Showcase
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions, 1428 Post Alley

Wednesdays from 8pm - 9:30pm Seattle’s only open mic for improv. Two-person improv teams! Double your pleasure, double the fun. Seattle’s one and only open mic dedicated to two-person improv teams! Every week at Unexpected Productions, two-person teams take the stage to create hilarious, fast-paced, totally unscripted comedy right before your eyes. No scripts, no safety nets—just pure improv magic. At Duos, everyone shines. Newer performers sharpen their game. Seasoned improvisers experiment, take risks, and get wonderfully weird. The results? Quick-witted scenes, surprising twists, and bold choices that feel impossible until you watch them happen live. Whether you dream of being the next Key & Peele, Tina Fey & Amy Poehler, or a chaotic tag-team like Deadpool & Wolverine, this is your chance to take the mic and run with it. All experience levels are welcome. Bring a friend, form a team, and give Seattle a show they won’t forget. To sign up your duo, email duoshowcase@gmail.com . Looki

Event details

item.title: The Original Food and Culture Tour of Pike Place Market
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Pike Place Market

VOTED 10 BEST FOOD TOURS IN THE COUNTRY BY USA TODAY! Join Savor Seattle for Seattle’s original food and cultural tour of Pike Place Market! On their signature tour, they will take you on a journey through the lively and bustling days in the heart of Seattle: Pike Place Market. While their guides give you the most delicious tastings at eight different partners. You’ll also learn the history of the Market and hear the stories of the small-batch food and craft producers that make this place one of Seattle’s most beloved landmarks. Find out why fish fly, why a pig is our mascot, how the “Original” Starbucks was founded, how an alleyway became covered in gum, what made Pike Place Market one of Anthony Bourdain’s favorite places, and many more pieces of info Seattle Savor guides we’ll be thrilled to share with you while you savor culinary offerings. Once you’re done, you’ll be equipped and ready to dive deeper into the historic neighborhood. Tours are available daily, starting at 10am and 2

Event details

And then some!!!” … These are some of the frequent comments we receive during our Market to Table experience!   Reserve Your Spot! Each experience INCLUDES: • 90-120 minutes VIP TOUR of Pike Place Market, visiting Chef Traci’s favorite spots, and includes some delightful sips and nibbles • 60-90 minute HANDS-ON cooking class (but if you want to sit and watch, you can do that too!) • 30-60 minute LUNCH or DINNER • All welcome nibbles, appetizers, non alcoholic beverages, tastes & sips along the tour, all ingredients for a customized cooking class • Estimate 3-4 hours for the complete experience • All class menus are created at the start of class and take into consideration individual dietary preferences and allergies (please note: the kitchen is not a gluten-free, nut-free facility, however menus may be customized to omit nuts, gluten, and/or dairy) The Market to Table tour and cooking cla

Event details

item.title: Loose Cannons Improv
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions, 1428 Post Alley

Thursdays from 8pm - 9:30pm Loose Cannons is Seattle improv at its most unpredictable. Every performance at Unexpected Productions in Pike Place Market is a one-of-a-kind experience: exciting, unexpected, a little dangerous, and always ridiculously fun. This is the show where anything can happen and usually does. Part One: Part One: The night kicks off with a unique performance, setting the performers in a new format with special guest improvisers showcasing their signature style or a never-before-seen format. Your suggestions fuel the fire, and the improvisers will ignite the stage, bringing your ideas to life. Part Two: The second half launches from a single audience suggestion, setting the performers in motion. It might start with a song, a monologue, or a rapid-fire group connection game. Whatever emerges will inspire spontaneous stories, surprising twists, and big comedic swings. Loose Cannons is fueled by audience ideas, fearless performers, and the thrill of not knowing what hap

Event details

item.title: Insider’s Breakfast and Culture Tour of Pike Place Market
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 8 AM

Location: Pike Place Market

VOTED 10 BEST FOOD TOURS IN THE COUNTRY BY USA TODAY! Get ahead of peak crowds and join Savor Seattle as they take you behind the scenes on an early-morning exploration of Pike Place Market. Their guides on this tour are deeply rooted members of the Market community who will truly take you to “Meet The Producer” using their years of experience in the neighborhood. This is an experience you cannot get with any regular tour guide! Feel like a local as you walk and eat your way through the best morning bites! Learn the names and stories of the people who operate the businesses Seattle Savor's is honored to highlight as their guides give you the full history of how Pike Place became The Soul of Seattle. "Watching the Market come to life is a time-honored Seattle tradition we would love for you to witness as we share our years of expertise with you. Come savor mornings at Pike Place Market!" Tours are available on Monday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday and Sunday from 8am - 10:30pm Book a Tour

Event details

item.title: The Original Food and Culture Tour of Pike Place Market
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Pike Place Market

VOTED 10 BEST FOOD TOURS IN THE COUNTRY BY USA TODAY! Join Savor Seattle for Seattle’s original food and cultural tour of Pike Place Market! On their signature tour, they will take you on a journey through the lively and bustling days in the heart of Seattle: Pike Place Market. While their guides give you the most delicious tastings at eight different partners. You’ll also learn the history of the Market and hear the stories of the small-batch food and craft producers that make this place one of Seattle’s most beloved landmarks. Find out why fish fly, why a pig is our mascot, how the “Original” Starbucks was founded, how an alleyway became covered in gum, what made Pike Place Market one of Anthony Bourdain’s favorite places, and many more pieces of info Seattle Savor guides we’ll be thrilled to share with you while you savor culinary offerings. Once you’re done, you’ll be equipped and ready to dive deeper into the historic neighborhood. Tours are available daily, starting at 10am and 2

Event details

item.title: 29th Annual Daffodil Day
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Pike Place Market, 85 Pike Street

Friday, March 20, 2026 11:00am – 2:00pm (or until supplies last) Celebrate the First Day of Spring at Pike Place Market’s 29th Annual Daffodil Day! Nothing says "spring has arrived" quite like the cheerful bloom of daffodils! Celebrate the changing of the seasons at Pike Place Market during the 29th Annual Daffodil Day on Friday, March 20. Stop by the Market’s famous Clock & Sign to pick up a free bundle of fresh, fragrant daffodils and learn more about the dedicated local flower farmers who grew them. These beautiful blooms, in vibrant shades of yellow, white, and orange, are a sure sign that sunnier days are ahead! Don't miss live busker music on the Farm Truck to celebrate the occasion, and be sure to stop by the PIKE BOX tent as we kick off registration for the upcoming CSA season. Make a day of it by strolling through the Market’s historic arcade, grabbing a bite to eat, or picking up a unique, locally made gift. Enjoy the sights, sounds, and flavors of Pike Place Market. All the

Event details

item.title: Improv Happy Hour
item.description:
Friday, March 20, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Unexpected Productions, 1428 Post Alley

Join us for Improv Happy Hour—where comedy is unscripted, unpredictable, and unbelievably fun! From movie mashups to literary spoofs and bold, experimental comedy, our quick-thinking performers turn your suggestions into hilarious scenes on the spot. This family-friendly show at the iconic Gum Wall in Pike Place Market guarantees a night of laughter, surprises, and improv magic you won’t forget. Bring your wildest ideas, your loudest laughs, and get ready for a one-of-a-kind comedy experience—because at Improv Happy Hour, anything can happen (and usually does)! Buy Tickets

Event details
